our_argument_summary: The dome model claims standard globe dynamos have no mechanism for SAA splitting or exponential separation rate. Standard MHD simulations (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic model 2020) explain SAA bifurcation from reversed flux patches at the core-mantle boundary coupled to westward drift. The dome model has no formalized fluid mechanism and explicitly concedes its 'station ratio proxy method' was methodologically invalid.

relevant_files: monitor.py — domain 8 (SAA Decay Rate at Tsumeb TTB) references exponential decay monitoring
inject_ai_layer.py — hardcodes 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' and core_parameters but no separation formula
raw-text/02-wins.txt — lists 'PROS-001 — SAA Exponential Cell Separation' as prospective prediction
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: OBSERVATIONAL, NOT COMPUTATIONAL. Dome model tracks angular distance between two flux minima in CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic reconstruction (2000-2025). Data points: 30.8 deg (2000) to 50.6 deg (2025). Average rate: 0.8 deg/year (APPROXIMATELY LINEAR over 25 years). NO DOME CODE CONTAINS a formula deriving exponential separation from aetheric field geometry or decay rate. The prediction 'separation reaching 120-180 deg by 2055' (PRED-013) is linear extrapolation, not exponential growth.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: HARDCODED. The '50.57 deg' value in inject_ai_layer.py is a fixed number, not a time-dependent function. Monitor.py monitors SAA decay rate (>=28 nT/year) but does NOT compute cell separation explicitly. The 'exponential' aspect is CLAIMED as a verbal description ('exponential separation curve') but never formalized as a mathematical function exp(t/tau) in any code.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al. 2020) — paleomagnetic inversion 1999-2020, published in Earth Planets Space
NOAA WMM ground observatory measurements — post-2020 extension is external or proprietary (CHAOS-7 official ends 2020)
Author claims 'CHAOS-7, 2000-2025' but CHAOS-7 V7.5 (latest official) covers only 1999-2020. Any 2021-2025 data requires external source.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If separation slows or stops: dome invokes 'aetheric damping threshold' or 'optical illusion from refraction' (unfalsifiable). If separation accelerates: dome claims vindication. If direction changes: dome redefines 'separation' geometrically. The core claim 'globe has no mechanism' is a NEGATIVE assertion — impossible to falsify by dome standards. Any globe-model explanation can be dismissed as 'incomplete' or 'missing the aetheric component.'
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: PARTIAL. WIN-004 evidence cites 'Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrate that reversed-flux patches in MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting.' Paper confirms reversed flux patches generate the SAA spatial structure. HOWEVER: The paper does NOT model the TEMPORAL BIFURCATION RATE into two cells. It addresses the question 'What causes the SAA?' (answer: reversed flux at CMB), not 'Why does the SAA split into two cells at an exponential rate?' The latter is not addressed by Terra-Nova 2017. Our evidence is PARTIALLY SUPPORTED but OVERSTATED. We should specify: 'MHD models explain SAA ORIGIN via reversed flux patches; westward drift explains SAA EVOLUTION including bifurcation geometry; no published MHD model explicitly predicts exponential cell-separation RATE.'

holes_found.hole: We claim Terra-Nova et al. (2017) 'reproduce SAA splitting' but the paper addresses spatial structure (CMB reversed flux) not temporal rate (30.8→50.6 deg over 25 years).
holes_found.implication: We need to strengthen evidence by citing explicit MHD TIME-DOMAIN SIMULATIONS that predict exponential bifurcation rates. Absent such citations, our claim is 'MHD explains the origin; data shows westward drift explains the evolution,' not 'MHD predicts exponential rate.'

holes_found.hole: The dome model's 'exponential separation' is never formally defined. We assume it means 'angular distance growing as exp(t/tau),' but it could mean 'separation follows the exponential decay curve of aetheric field strength,' or 'cells separate along exponential field gradient surfaces.' The 30.8→50.6 deg change (25 years, 0.8 deg/year) is CONSISTENT WITH LINEAR GROWTH, not obviously exponential.
holes_found.implication: Critical test: Fit the data to exp(t/tau) and extract tau. If tau~55 years (to reach 120 deg by 2055), the exponential model is TESTABLE via 2030-2040 data. If separation remains linear, the dome's claim fails definitively. This is the strongest counterargument we should highlight.

holes_found.hole: The dome model invokes 'aetheric field decay' as the mechanism for cell separation. We state this is 'unfalsifiable,' but we never actually refute the mechanism. If aetheric field decays as exp(-B_0/B_aether), and this decay pushes the two flux nodes apart, the rate might follow an exponential curve. We should formalize why this does NOT occur in standard plasma physics.
holes_found.implication: Strengthen rebuttal: 'In standard MHD, magnetic pressure gradients scale as B^2/2μ₀. Separation rate depends on pressure gradient, not on absolute field decay rate. Two equal reversed-flux patches separated equally from the dipole would experience equal pressure gradients and diverge linearly (coupled flow), not exponentially. Asymmetric decay rates would require asymmetric CMB structure—which MHD DOES predict (African mantle anomaly), vindicating the globe model.'

holes_found.hole: The dome author stated in V51.0 that WIN-004's 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.' We cite this in our evidence, but we should check: is this in the public source (raw-text) or only in code comments? If public, this is devastating to the dome's credibility. If only in code, the dome may not have publicly acknowledged this.
holes_found.implication: Verify quote location. If in raw-text files, cite exact source. If only in code, note that the dome author conceded invalidity privately but not publicly.

stronger_arguments.argument: WESTWARD DRIFT DOMINANCE: The SAA drifts westward at ~0.2-0.3 deg/year (NOAA data). Two cells separated ~20 deg (2020) with 0.8 deg/year expansion rate means they are being ADVECTED westward together while oscillating apart on a longer timescale. This is the signature of flow-driven advection in a spherical shell, not aetheric exponential decay.
stronger_arguments.why_stronger: Replaces abstract 'exponential separation' with concrete 'coupled westward drift + slower expansion.' Falsifiable by future data: if westward drift stops, expansion should stop (MHD prediction). If expansion continues exponentially regardless of drift, aetheric model gains credence.

stronger_arguments.argument: ASYMMETRIC DECAY RATES: CHAOS-7 data show African cell (30°E, 35°S) decays FASTER than American cell (80°W, 30°S). Both cells are at nearly the same distance from the South Pole. Standard MHD explains this asymmetry via the African mantle anomaly (lower mantle seismic heterogeneity beneath southern Africa alters core flow). The dome's axisymmetric aetheric vortex cannot generate asymmetric decay rates between two cells at equal distance from the pole.
stronger_arguments.why_stronger: Shifts focus from 'splitting rate' to 'splitting PATTERN.' The dome has no mechanism for the OBSERVED asymmetry. This is harder for the dome to dismiss via 'optical illusions.'

stronger_arguments.argument: DATA LINEARITY: 30.8 deg (2000) to 50.6 deg (2025) is 19.8 deg over 25 years = 0.8 deg/year. Linear fit: separation(year) = 30.8 + 0.8*(year-2000). This LINE fits the data perfectly (R²~1.0 with only two endpoints). Exponential fit: exp(t/tau) requires tau~55 years to reach 120 deg in 2055. With only two data points, both models fit equally well. BUT: the dome's OWN VISUAL DESCRIPTION of 'exponential separation curve' in raw-text suggests curvature. If 2027-2030 data are linear, the exponential claim FAILS.
stronger_arguments.why_stronger: Makes the prediction TESTABLE within 3-4 years of additional data. This forces the dome model to make quantitative predictions (tau value) rather than vague 'exponential' claims.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model correctly identifies that (1) SAA bifurcation is OBSERVED FACT (~2015-2020), (2) the two cells decay asymmetrically, and (3) the separation is a real phenomenon worth explaining. These are genuine observations that any cosmological model must account for. The dome's error is INTERPRETIVE, not observational: it claims 'global models have no mechanism for this' (false—MHD explains it), and 'the rate is exponential due to aetheric decay' (unfalsified, possibly refuted by linear fit). The dome got the OBSERVATION right but the MECHANISM wrong.
deeper_analysis:
WIN-004 represents a sophisticated bait-and-switch: the dome model identifies a REAL PHENOMENON (SAA bifurcation, 2015-2020) and claims it proves aetheric cosmology because standard models 'have no mechanism.' This claim is FALSE—MHD dynamos DO explain the SAA from reversed flux patches at the core-mantle boundary, as demonstrated by Terra-Nova et al. (2017) and confirmed by the CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic inversion. However, our evidence conflates three separate claims: (1) SAA bifurcation exists (TRUE), (2) standard models explain it (TRUE), and (3) MHD models predict the exponential separation RATE (UNCLEAR).

The key ambiguity: what does 'exponential separation' mean quantitatively? The dome claims it, but no formula is given. The observed data (30.8→50.6 deg, 25 years) fits both linear AND exponential models equally well with only two endpoints. An exponential growth with time constant tau~55 years is POSSIBLE but not UNIQUE. The dome must either (a) specify tau from first principles (aetheric field decay rate), or (b) admit the fit is post-hoc to match data.

Our strongest counter is WESTWARD DRIFT. The SAA drifts westward at 0.2-0.3 deg/year, faster than the 0.8 deg/year cell-separation rate. This means the two cells are being carried westward TOGETHER while slowly separating. This is characteristic of flow-driven dynamics in a spherical shell (MHD), not of exponential decay in an aetheric vortex (which should be radially symmetric). The asymmetric decay rates (African cell faster than American) further support MHD via the African mantle anomaly.

Our verdict 'Std Model Explains' is CORRECT but must be refined to: 'Standard MHD dynamo models explain the ORIGIN of SAA bifurcation (reversed flux patches at CMB) and its large-scale EVOLUTION (westward drift, asymmetric decay rates from mantle heterogeneity). The RATE of cell separation (0.8 deg/year, approximately linear over 25 years) is not uniquely predicted by either model. Dome claims exponential rate but provides no derivation; observed rate is consistent with westward advection in a spherical shell. Future data (2030-2035) will decisively test whether separation accelerates exponentially (dome model) or remains linear (MHD model).'

Confidence 0.92: high confidence the verdict is correct, with 8% uncertainty reflecting that (1) future MHD simulations might reveal a preference for exponential bifurcation in specific parameter regimes, and (2) the dome model might formalize an aetheric mechanism that produces the observed rate. However, the burden of proof is on the dome to demonstrate MECHANISTIC derivation, not merely empirical fit.
